facts.text: The Maya civilization flourished in parts of present-day Mexico, Guatemala, Belize, Honduras, and El Salvador.
The Maya built impressive city-states such as Tikal, Palenque, Copán, and Chichén Itzá.
Maya civilization is known for its advances in astronomy, mathematics, architecture, and writing.
The Maya developed one of the most sophisticated writing systems in the ancient Americas.
Maya mathematicians used the concept of zero centuries before it became common in Europe.
The Maya calendar system was highly advanced and contributed to the civilization's reputation for precise astronomy.
Maya rulers often used temples and monuments to connect political authority with religion and cosmic order.
The famous stepped pyramid at Chichén Itzá is also known as El Castillo.
Maya ballgames had ritual and political importance as well as athletic competition.
The Popol Vuh preserves important myths and traditions from the K'iche' Maya world.
Rather than one unified empire, the Maya world was made up of many interacting and competing city-states.
Many Maya cities experienced decline during the Classic period collapse, but Maya peoples and cultures continued and still thrive today.
Modern Maya communities preserve languages, traditions, and cultural identities across Mesoamerica.
The Maya civilization became one of the great cultures of ancient Mesoamerica.
Its cities, writing, and astronomy helped make it one of the most studied civilizations in the Americas.
Maya civilization is one of the standout topics people often explore within Mayans.
Readers interested in Mayans regularly come across Maya civilization.
Learning about Maya civilization helps explain part of the lasting appeal of Mayans.
Maya civilization remains one of the most recognizable examples connected with Mayans.
Articles, documentaries, and fan discussions about Mayans often highlight Maya civilization.
For many people, Maya civilization is a memorable entry point into Mayans.
The story of Maya civilization helps show why Mayans stays so interesting to revisit.
